I think what's exciting here is all the different people coming from around the world to showcase their art in the classic Biennale. And so we were very deliberate about staging our finale at the same time here, because in a way, bright moments is an

international community of artists, mostly gendered artists and AI artists who are coming together for this final gathering to share time with each other, to reflect upon the last three years of bright moments and to share some new work with each other and with our collectors.

Like if I think about where we started in Venice Beach and then New York and then Berlin, every step of the way, I think we tried to up our game in terms of cultural awareness, in terms of our own technology, in terms of the artist we're working with, in terms of, yeah, just our own kind of sophistication in terms of doing what we're doing and putting on really interesting experiences. And so I think from London to Mexico, I think Mexico was really where we really opened up this idea of workshops and panels and we did, you know, hands-on workshops with KCREs and Zach Lieberman and we did a panel with conversation with Gann Brude and Sophia Cresco about AI art.
